mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance with p-value greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand recovery that drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production above the 2017 baseline index level confirms operational capacity utilization that supports near-term equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 50% of observations when INDPRO exceeds 100, or predictive power vanishes after controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers inflation fears that force anticipated monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in more than 65% of high-inflation months, or the signal shows zero incremental predictive value over a constant baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment rates indicates structural labor market weakness that suppresses earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from independent main effects in panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic output that directly boosts corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows, or GDP growth periods produce negative equity returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production decline exceeding 2.0 percent signals acute supply chain disruption that forces immediate equity repricing and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of severe contraction months, or the signal loses predictive accuracy after controlling for concurrent monetary policy shifts.
